items.front: Converging runway wake intervals: when do they apply?
items.back: Apply the listed time intervals when aircraft take off behind a landing or departing aircraft on a converging runway and projected flight paths will cross.

items.front: Converging runway wake interval: can pilot request a deviation?
items.back: No. Do not approve deviation from the required time interval if the preceding aircraft requires wake turbulence separation.

items.front: Converging runway projected paths: Category B-H behind Category A
items.back: Use a 3-minute interval.

items.front: Altitude assignment at/below FL 410: course 000-179
items.back: Odd cardinal altitudes or flight levels at 2,000-foot intervals.

items.front: Altitude assignment at/below FL 410: course 180-359
items.back: Even cardinal altitudes or flight levels at 2,000-foot intervals.

items.front: Altitude assignment above FL 410: course 000-179
items.back: Odd cardinal flight levels at 4,000-foot intervals beginning with FL 450.

items.front: Altitude assignment above FL 410: course 180-359
items.back: Odd cardinal flight levels at 4,000-foot intervals beginning with FL 430.

items.front: Minimum flight level adjustment: 29.91 to 29.42 inches
items.back: Add 500 feet to the flight-level equivalent of the minimum altitude.

items.front: Minimum flight level adjustment: 29.41 to 28.92 inches
items.back: Add 1,000 feet to the flight-level equivalent of the minimum altitude.

items.front: Minimum flight level adjustment: 28.91 to 28.42 inches
items.back: Add 1,500 feet to the flight-level equivalent of the minimum altitude.

items.front: Minimum flight level adjustment: 28.41 to 27.92 inches
items.back: Add 2,000 feet to the flight-level equivalent of the minimum altitude.

items.front: Higher MEA ahead: no MCA specified
items.back: Clear the aircraft to begin climb prior to or immediately after passing the fix where the higher MEA is designated.

items.front: Higher MEA ahead: MCA specified
items.back: Clear the aircraft to climb before the fix so it crosses the fix at or above the MCA.

items.front: Cleared below MEA but not below MOCA: VOR/VORTAC/TACAN navigation
items.back: Only within 22 miles of the NAVAID, and the assigned altitude must be at least 300 feet above controlled-airspace floor.

items.front: Parallel departure/arrival: departure-course divergence
items.back: Departure course must diverge immediately by at least 30 degrees from the missed approach course until separation is applied.

items.front: Parallel thresholds even: runway centerline spacing
items.back: Runway centerlines must be at least 2,500 feet apart.

items.front: Parallel thresholds staggered: arrival approaching nearer runway
items.back: Centerlines must be at least 1,000 feet apart, and thresholds must be staggered at least 500 feet for each 100 feet less than 2,500 feet of centerline spacing.

items.front: Nonintersecting diverging runways
items.back: Runways must diverge by 15 degrees or more and runway edges must not touch.

items.front: Simultaneous dependent approaches: turn-on separation
items.back: Provide at least 1,000 feet vertical or 3 miles radar separation between aircraft during turn-on.

items.front: Dependent parallel finals: 2,500 to 3,600 feet centerline spacing
items.back: Use at least 1 mile diagonal radar separation between successive aircraft on adjacent final approach courses.

items.front: Dependent parallel finals: more than 3,600 to 8,300 feet centerline spacing
items.back: Use at least 1.5 miles diagonal radar separation between successive aircraft on adjacent final approach courses.

items.front: Dependent parallel finals: more than 8,300 to 9,000 feet centerline spacing
items.back: Use at least 2 miles diagonal radar separation between successive aircraft on adjacent final approach courses.

items.front: Departing/arriving same airport: takeoff direction differs at least 45 degrees
items.back: Departure must take off 3 minutes before the arrival is estimated at the airport when using the time-based nonradar minimum.

items.front: Departing/arriving same airport: other takeoff direction
items.back: Departure must be established on a course diverging at least 45 degrees from the reciprocal final approach course 5 minutes before the arrival is estimated at the airport or before it starts procedure turn.

items.front: Terminal departing/arriving same airport: fix-based 45-degree case
items.back: Departure takes off before the arrival leaves a fix inbound not less than 4 miles from the airport.

items.front: Opposite-course nonradar separation: default vertical window
items.back: Assign approved vertical separation from 10 minutes before until 10 minutes after the aircraft are estimated to pass.

items.front: Opposite-course RNAV/RNAV discontinuation: route width 8 miles or less
items.back: After both report passing the same position and are at least 8 miles apart.

items.front: Opposite-course RNAV/RNAV discontinuation: route segment wider than 8 miles
items.back: After both report passing the same position and are at least 18 miles apart, unless the extended-distance case requires 30 miles.

items.front: Opposite-course same intersection/waypoint report
items.back: Vertical separation may be discontinued after both aircraft pass the same intersection/waypoint and are at least 3 minutes apart.

items.front: Route protected-airspace reduction below FL 240
items.back: 5 miles each side to 57.14 miles from the NAVAID, then expanding on a 5-degree angle.

items.front: Route protected-airspace reduction at/above FL 240
items.back: 10 miles each side to 114.29 miles from the NAVAID, then expanding on a 5-degree angle.

items.front: Before reducing route protected airspace
items.back: The routes must have been satisfactorily flight checked and notice must have been given to users.

items.front: Offshore/oceanic boundary below FL 180: QNE/QNH issue
items.back: When vertical separation is applied, take control action so standard/local altimeter-setting differences do not compromise separation.

items.front: Offshore to oceanic transition: opposite-direction climb-through
items.back: Vertical separation above the opposite-direction oceanic traffic must be established before crossing the offshore/oceanic boundary and 15 minutes before estimated passing.

items.front: Opposite-direction offshore/oceanic transition: boundary condition
items.back: Vertical separation above the oceanic traffic must exist before the outbound crosses the offshore/oceanic boundary.

items.front: Opposite-direction offshore/oceanic transition: timing condition
items.back: Vertical separation above the oceanic traffic must exist 15 minutes before the aircraft are estimated to pass.

items.front: Temporary stationary reservation: lateral separation
items.back: Clear the aircraft so protected airspace along the route of flight does not overlap the geographical area of the reservation.

items.front: Temporary stationary reservation: vertical separation area
items.back: Maintain vertical separation while the aircraft is within the reservation plus a perimeter buffer equal to one-half the lateral separation minimum.
